name: Rough-in: receptacle opening
description: Rough-in wiring and box for one receptacle opening during open-wall construction (per opening).
labor_notes: Open framing makes this fast: box set, cable staple-run from the nearest daisy point, nail plates where required.

name: Rough-in: switch opening
description: Rough-in wiring and box for one switch opening during open-wall construction (per opening).
labor_notes: Comparable to a receptacle opening; multi-gang locations should count one opening per gang.

name: Rough-in: lighting opening
description: Rough-in wiring and ceiling box for one lighting outlet during open construction (per opening).
labor_notes: Ceiling boxes take slightly longer than wall boxes for blocking and overhead cable dressing.

name: Rough-in: home-run circuit (per circuit)
description: Home-run cable from the panel to the first opening of a new circuit, including breaker (installed at trim).
labor_notes: Per-circuit price for the panel-to-first-box run and later breaker landing; distance beyond a typical run should use the long-run modifier.

name: Kitchen remodel rough-in package
description: Complete electrical rough-in for a typical kitchen remodel: small-appliance circuits, dedicated appliance circuits, lighting, and range feed.
labor_notes: A code-compliant kitchen carries 7-9 circuits plus lighting layout; two days with a helper covers boxes, home runs, and inspection prep. Larger kitchens should add per-opening lines.

name: Bath remodel rough-in package
description: Complete electrical rough-in for a bathroom remodel: 20A GFCI-protected receptacle circuit, lighting, fan/heater wiring.
labor_notes: Dedicated bath circuit, vanity and shower-rated lighting boxes, and fan wiring; a focused day with helper support for the fan duct path.

name: Trim-out: receptacle opening
description: Install device and plate at one roughed-in receptacle opening (per opening).
labor_notes: Straight production work at trim: make up, terminate, plate, and test each opening.

name: Trim-out: switch opening
description: Install switch and plate at one roughed-in switch opening (per opening).
labor_notes: Same production rate as receptacle trim; dimmers or smart devices should use their own assemblies.

name: Trim-out: lighting opening
description: Connect and hang the fixture at one roughed-in lighting opening (per opening, standard fixtures).
labor_notes: Covers connection and mounting of typical customer-selected fixtures; heavy or high fixtures move to their specific assemblies.

name: Rough-in: smoke/CO detector opening
description: Rough-in a ceiling box and interconnect wiring for one hardwired smoke/CO location (per opening).
labor_notes: Interconnect (14/3) between detector locations is what separates this from a plain lighting opening.

name: Rough-in: 240V appliance opening
description: Rough-in wiring and box for one 240V appliance location (range, dryer, or similar) during open construction.
labor_notes: Heavy-gauge cable handling and a two-gang box; per-opening price assumes the panel run is counted as its own home-run line.

name: Remodel panel make-ready
description: Land new remodel circuits in the panel with correct AFCI/GFCI protection, re-label the directory, and prep for inspection.
labor_notes: Batch-landing several new circuits with the right protective breakers and a clean directory; inspection-day availability is included in the allowance of time.
